steps.title: Match the given root to the quadratic formula
steps.content:
The answer choices are about the value of $c$, and the given solution already has the shape of a quadratic-formula result. For $3w^2 + 13w - c = 0$, the coefficients are $a=3$, $b=13$, and the constant term is $-c$. The quadratic formula gives:
$$w = \frac{-13 \pm \sqrt{13^2 - 4(3)(-c)}}{2(3)}$$
Simplify the expression under the square root:
$$w = \frac{-13 \pm \sqrt{169 + 12c}}{6}$$

steps.title: Extract the discriminant from the given solution
steps.content:
The given solution $\frac{-13+\sqrt{409}}{6}$ matches our formula structure exactly. By comparing with $\frac{-13 \pm \sqrt{169 + 12c}}{6}$, the term under the square root must be 409:
$$169 + 12c = 409$$
